title: 10X Genomics Single-Nucleus RNA-Sequencing for Transcriptomic Profiling of Adult Human Tissues
author: Sarah Urata
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.86khzcw

steps.title: Resuspend Nuclei
steps.instruction: Resuspend nuclei in 100 µL to 1 mL of PBS + 0.1% RNase Inhibitor (volume depends on target concentration)

steps.title: Count Nuclei
steps.instruction: Count nuclei (e.g. BioRad T20 Cell Counter)

steps.title: Check Nuclei Integrity
steps.instruction: Check nuclei integrity under fluorescent microscope using DAPI channel. Nuclei should appear distinct, have rounded borders and the majority occurring as singlets. High clumping rates would indicate damaged nuclei and would require re-filtering using 30-µm CellTrics filter or exclusion.

steps.title: Prepare Reagents for GEM Generation
steps.instruction: Prepare Reagents for use Equilibrate gel beads to room temperature for 00:30:00 . Thaw, vortex, and centrifuge RT Reagent, Template Switch Oligo, and Reducing Agent B. Verify no precipitate in Reducing Agent B. Centrifuge RT Enzyme C before adding to Master Mix.

steps.title: Prepare Master Mix
steps.instruction: Prepare on ice. Pipette mix 15x and centrifuge briefly. Add 33.4 µL Master Mix to each tube of a PCR 8-tube strip on ice.

steps.title: Assemble Chromium Chip B in a 10X Chip Holder
steps.instruction: Assemble Chromium Chip B in a 10X Chip Holder.

steps.title: Must load chip in order according...
steps.instruction: Must load chip in order according to row label for microfluidic channels to work properly: Row 1 → Row 2 → Row 3

steps.title: Make sure NO bubbles are introduced while loading chip
steps.instruction: Make sure NO bubbles are introduced while loading chip.

steps.title: Dispense 50% glycerol solution into unused...
steps.instruction: Dispense 50% glycerol solution into unused Chip Wells (if <8 samples used per chip) a. 75 µL into unused wells in Row 1 b. 40 µL into unused wells in Row 2 c. 280 µL into unused wells in Row 3

steps.title: Use Cell Suspension Volume Calculator Table...
steps.instruction: Use Cell Suspension Volume Calculator Table to add the appropriate volume of nuclease-free water and corresponding volume of nuclei stock to make working nuclei suspension 6.Nuclei Loading a.Minimum: load 800 nuclei → target recovery 500 nuclei b.Maximum: load 1600 nuclei → target recovery 10000 nuclei 7.Add 46.6 µL working Nuclei Suspension to the Master Mix in PCR tubes on ice for 80 µL total volume 8.Load 75 µL Master Mix and Nuclei Suspension into the bottom center of each well in Row 1 without introducing bubbles. 9.Snap the Gel Bead strip into a 10X Vortex Adapter. Vortex 00:00:30 . Remove the Gel Bead strip and flick sharply downward to ensure maximum recovery. Confirm there are no bubbles at the bottom of the tubes and liquid levels look even. 10.Load 40 µL gel beads in Row

steps.title: Only puncture the foil seal for...
steps.instruction: Only puncture the foil seal for gel bead tubes being used. Dispense slowly and without introducing bubbles. 11.Load 280 µL partitioning oil into each Row 3 by pipetting two aliquots of 140 µL . 12.Attach 10X gasket. Align the top-notch. Ensure gasket holes are aligned with the wells. Avoid touching the smooth gasket surface. Do not press down on the gasket.

steps.title: Run Chromium Controller
steps.instruction: Run the Chromium Controller Press the eject button on the controller to eject. Place the assembled chip with the gasket in the tray. Press the button to retract the tray. Confirm the program on screen. Press the play button. At completion of the run (~ 00:07:00 ), the controller will chime. Proceed immediately to the next step.

steps.title: Transfer GEMs
steps.instruction: Transfer GEMs Chill strip tubes on ice. Press the eject button to remove the chip. Discard the gasket. Open the chip holder. Fold the lid back until it clicks to expose the wells at 45 degrees. Check the volume in rows 1-3. Abnormally high volume in any well indicates a clog. Slowly aspirate 100 uL GEMs from the lowest points of the Recovery Wells without creating a seal between the pipette tips and the wells. GEMs should appear opaque and uniform across all channels. Excess partitioning oil (clear) in the pipette tips indicates a potential clog. 6. Slowly dispense (~ 00:00:20 ) GEMs into the tube strip on ice with the pipette tips against the sidewalls of the wells.

steps.title: GEM-RT Incubation
steps.instruction: GEM-RT Incubation Incubate in a thermocycler to complete reverse transcription. Store at 4 °C for up to 72:00:00 or at -20 °C for up to a week. Or proceed to the next step.

steps.title: Prepare Reagents for Post GEM-RT Cleanup
steps.instruction: Post GEM-RT Cleanup & cDNA Amplification - Prepare Reagents for use Thaw, vortex, and centrifuge a tube of Reducing Agent B and cDNA primers. Maintain Amp Mix on ice after vortex and centrifuge Thaw Cleanup Buffer at 65 °C for 00:10:00 with shaking at max rpm then cool to room temperature.

steps.title: Dynabead Cleanup
steps.instruction: Post GEM RT-Cleanup --Dynabead Add 125 µL Recovery Agent to each sample at room temperature. DO NOT MIX. Wait 00:01:00 . Carefully aspirate 125 µL of pink oil phase from the bottom of the tube. DO NOT aspirate any aqueous sample. Prepare Dynabeads Cleanup Mix Vortex and centrifuge mixture and add 200 µL to each sample. Pipette 5x to mix. Incubate at room temperature for 00:10:00 ; pipette mix again 00:05:00 into incubation. Prepare Elution Solution I. Vortex and centrifuge briefly. Place sample on magnetic separator and remove the supernatant. Wash twice with freshly prepared 80% ethanol. Elute with 35.5 uL Elution Solution I. Transfer 35 uL to a new strip tube.

steps.title: cDNA Amplification
steps.instruction: cDNA Amplification Prepare cDNA Amplification Mix on ice. Vortex and centrifuge briefly. Add 65 µL cDNA Amplification Reaction Mix to 35 µL sample. Pipette mix 15x to mix and centrifuge briefly. Incubate in a thermocycler to amplify cDNA. Use cycle number optimization table for total number of cycles When using nuclei, increase the optimized number of cycles by one cycle Maximum: load 1600 nuclei - perform 12 cycles

steps.title: cDNA Cleanup with SPRIselect
steps.instruction: cDNA Cleanup --SPRIselect Perform a 0.6X beads purification with SPRIselect reagent Wash twice with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elute with 40.5 µL Qiagen Buffer EB. Transfer 40 µL to a new strip tube. Store at 4 °C for up to 72:00:00 or at -20 °C for up to 4 weeks. Or proceed to the next step.

steps.title: cDNA QC & Quantification
steps.instruction: cDNA QC & Quantification Run TapeStation to obtain concentration and size Expected size range for amplified cDNA: 400 to 2500 bp cDNA total yield range: 80 to 1000 ng QC Cutoff: Minimum cDNA total yield of 80 ng

steps.title: Prepare Reagents for Library Construction
steps.instruction: Prepare Reagents for use Thaw, vortex, and centrifuge Fragmentation Buffer, Adaptor Oligos, Ligation Buffer, SI Primer. Maintain on ice Fragmentation Enzyme, DNA Ligase, and Amp Mix.

steps.title: Prepare Fragmentation Mix on ice
steps.instruction: Prepare Fragmentation Mix on ice. Pipette mix and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Transfer 10 µL purified cDNA to...
steps.instruction: Transfer 10 µL purified cDNA to a new strip tube Note that 10 uL (25%) cDNA sample is sufficient for generating 3' Gene Expression Library. The remaining 30 uL (75%) cDNA sample can be stored at 4 C for up to 72 hours or at -20 C for up to 4 weeks for generating additional 3' Gene Expression Libraries.

steps.title: Add 25 µL Buffer EB to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL Buffer EB to each sample

steps.title: Add 15 µL Fragmentation Mix to each sample
steps.instruction: Add 15 µL Fragmentation Mix to each sample

steps.title: Pipette mix, centrifuge briefly, and transfer to pre-cooled block
steps.instruction: Pipette mix, centrifuge briefly, and transfer to pre-cooled block. “SKIP” hold step to initiate the protocol

steps.title: Double Sided Size Selection with SPRIselect
steps.instruction: Post Fragmentation, End Repair & A-tailing Double Sided Size Selection --SPRIselect Vortex to resuspend the SPRIselect reagent. Add 30 µL SPRIselect reagent (0.6X) to each sample and pipette mix 15x. Incubate 00:05:00 at room temperature. Centrifuge briefly Place sample on magnetic separator. DO NOT discard supernatant. Transfer 75 µL supernatant to a new tube strip tube. Vortex to resuspend the SPRIselect reagent. Add 10 µL SPRIselect reagent (0.8X) to each sample and pipette mix 15x. Place sample on magnetic separator and remove supernatant. DO NOT discard any beads. Wash twice with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elute with 50.5 µL Qiagen Buffer EB. Transfer 50 µL to a new strip tube.

steps.title: Adaptor Ligation
steps.instruction: Adaptor Ligation Prepare Adaptor Ligation Mix on ice. Pipette mix and centrifuge briefly. Add 50 µL Adaptor Ligation Mix to sample. Pipette mix 15x. Centrifuge briefly. Incubate in a thermocycler to ligase adaptor.

steps.title: Post Ligation Cleanup with SPRIselect
steps.instruction: Post Ligation Cleanup --SPRIselect Perform a 0.8X beads purification with SPRIselect reagent Wash twice with freshly prepared 80% ethanol Elute with 30.5 µL Qiagen Buffer EB. Transfer 30 µL to a new strip tube.

steps.title: Choose the appropriate sample index sets...
steps.instruction: Choose the appropriate sample index sets to ensure that no sample indices overlap in a multiplexed sequencing run.

steps.title: Prepare Sample Index PCR Mix on ice
steps.instruction: Prepare Sample Index PCR Mix on ice. Pipette mix and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Add 60 µL Sample Index PCR Mix to the sample
steps.instruction: Add 60 µL Sample Index PCR Mix to the sample

steps.title: Add 10 µL of individual Chromium...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of individual Chromium i7 Sample Index (from SI-GA plate) to each well. Record well assignment. Mix and centrifuge briefly.

steps.title: Incubate in a thermocycler to incorporate sample indices
steps.instruction: Incubate in a thermocycler to incorporate sample indices. Use cycle number recommendation table for total number of cycles; recommended number of cycles is based on cDNA input

steps.title: Store at 4 °C for up to 72:00:00
steps.instruction: Store at 4 °C for up to 72:00:00 . Or proceed to the next step.
